objective: Fully implement Alchemy Dev Agent System from specification into a usable autonomous development runtime, including real Codex worker adapter, retry/debug loop, task graph scheduling, evaluation gates, GitHub execution flow, tests, docs, and keep iterating until done or blocked.

tests_run.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python -B -m runtime.run_loop --project .test-tmp/<unique-smoke-dir> --objective "build a todo app with login" --reset
tests_run.summary: CLI smoke reached done=true, status=passed, final_gate_score=0.94.

tests_run.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python -B -m runtime.run_loop --project .test-tmp/final-smoke --objective "build a todo app with login" --reset
tests_run.summary: Final CLI smoke reached done=true, status=passed, final_gate_score=0.94.

known_bugs: Windows sandbox denies Python bytecode writes to existing __pycache__; verification uses PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and python -B.
This environment reports access denied when launching the installed Codex app alias, so real Codex execution is implemented and subprocess-tested but not live-smoke-tested here.
done_criteria: Real Codex worker adapter exists with structured worker package and result parsing.
Retry/debug loop and task graph scheduling are implemented.
Evaluation gate enforces weighted dimensions and hard failures.
GitHub execution flow records dry-run evidence and supports real git/gh mode.
Relevant unit tests and CLI smoke pass.
README and docs describe runtime usage.
